title: 4 Reporting CEBA Loan Repayments
body_text:
4	Reporting CEBA Loan Repayments
4.1	Overview of Reporting CEBA Loan Repayments
Export Development Canada (EDC) requires that loan repayments are reported to Central 1 and submitted to EDC twice a month. The reporting schedule for loan repayments is shown in the table below.
Customer Makes Payment to Financial Institution
Financial Institution Reports Payments to Central 1
Between the 1st and the 15th of the month
By the end of the 22nd day of the same month
Between the 16th and the last day of the month
By the end of the 7th day of the
following
month
Central 1 will debit your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account for the reported amounts on the 8th and the 23rd day each month and submit the funds to EDC on the same day.
4.2	Completing the CEBA Repayment Form in ServiceNow
Use the CEBA Repayment form in ServiceNow to report loan payments. To complete the CEBA Repayment form:
Log in to ServiceNow as described in Section 3.1, Logging in to ServiceNow.
Select
CEBA Program - Repayment
in the Paper Payments category. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Selecting CEBA Program ГўВҖВ“ Repayment Form
Complete the top section of the
CEBA Program ГўВҖВ“ Repayment
form as shown in Figure 2:
ГўВ‘
Reporting Month
ГўВҖВ“ Select the month in which you are reporting a repayment.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Reporting
Period
ГўВҖВ“ Is pre-populated after you select the reporting month.
ГўВ‘Вў
CEBA Loan Application
ГўВҖВ“
Select the CEBA loan application from the drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Loan Type
ГўВҖВ“
Defaults to Term Loan.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Loan Drawdowns Date
ГўВҖВ“
Reflects the date for loan drawdowns for the applicant.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Loan Drawdowns Amount
ГўВҖВ“
For term loans, the drawdown amount is pre-populated with the requested amount of the loan.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Credit Union
ГўВҖВ“
Your credit unionГўВҖВҷs name.
ГўВ‘В§
Borrower Name
ГўВҖВ“ Your business memberГўВҖВҷs company name.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
CRA Business Number
ГўВҖВ“ BorrowerГўВҖВҷs business number.
ГўВ‘В©
Loan Available Date
ГўВҖВ“ Date when loan became available.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Loan Amount Available
ГўВҖВ“ Funding amount made available to borrower.
ГўВ‘В«
Forgiveness Amount Limit
ГўВҖВ“ Forgivable amount (based on the total amount borrowed) if loan is repaid in full by December 31, 2023.
Figure 2: Top Section of CEBA Program ГўВҖВ“ Repayment Form
Complete the Repayment Details (bottom section) of the
CEBA Program ГўВҖВ“ Repayment
form as shown in Figure 3:
ГўВ‘
Type of Payment
ГўВҖВ“ Select the type of payment from the drop-down menu:
ГўВҲВ’
Principal Payments from Borrower
means the amount that the borrower pays towards the principal amount of the loan. When a borrower makes a payment towards the principal, your financial institution must remit the same amount to CentralВ 1 within 15 business days.
ГўВҲВ’
Loan Interest Payments Received from Borrower
means the amount of interest that the borrower paid within a reporting period (only applicable if borrower has not repaid the principal amount of the loan on or before December 31, 2023).
ГўВҲВ’
Principal Forgiveness Refund Due to Overpayment
ГўВҖВ“ This data element should only be used if a customer makes a payment more than the non-forgivable portion, not realizing they are entitled to forgiveness. If the customer requests a refund of the applicable forgiveness amount, the amount is entered in this data element.
This data element is not to enable refunds or reversals of principal payments in any other case
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Payment
Date
ГўВҖВ“ Select the date when payment was made (the calendar allows you to select dates from previous months).
ГўВ‘Вў
Payment
Amount
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount that was paid on the Payment Date.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select the plus sign to add another row for additional repayment details if your customer made more than one payment in the reporting period.
Scenario
ГўВҖВ“ Borrower made a $500 payment on October 4, 2021, and a $700 payment on October 13, 2021. If you need to report a payment from a previous reporting period, you have until the 7th of the current month to select the applicable payment date from the calendar. If itГўВҖВҷs past the 7th, report the payment in the current reporting period.
Type of Payment
Payment Date
Payment Amount
Principal Payments from Borrower
2021-10-04
$500
Principal Payments from Borrower
2021-10-13
$700
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Outstanding balance after this payment
ГўВҖВ“ Outstanding principal on loan amount after this payment. If reporting forgiveness ($10K or $20K) or fully repaying the loan ($40K or $60K without forgiveness), enter $0 in this field.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Loan status after this payment
ГўВҖВ“ Select an option from the drop-down menu to indicate where the loan is within its lifecycle:
Active, no late interest payments
Fully Repaid
Active, bearing interest, later interest payments (after December 31, 2023)
Cessation event triggered, demand not yet issued/bankruptcy not yet filed
Demand letter issued and/or claim in bankruptcy filed by the FI
Initial collections period ended (Non-Borrowing only)
Assignment to the lender
Assignment to 3rd party collections
Write-off
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Forgiveness Amount Credited ГўВҖВ“ Date
ГўВҖВ“ The date of the forgiveness should be the same as the date of the final loan payment. To qualify for forgiveness, this date must be on or before January 18, 2024, or loan holders who applied for refinancing can benefit from an extended forgiveness deadline of March 28, 2024. Forgiveness canГўВҖВҷt be credited until the full amount of the loan is paid. Leave this field blank if loan is not repaid in full before the deadline.
ГўВ‘В§
Principal Forgiveness Amount ГўВҖВ“ Claimed at Time of Repayment
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the Forgiveness Amount. Forgiveness can only be claimed once. It should be reported in the same reporting period when you report your customerГўВҖВҷs final payment on the loan. Leave this field blank if the forgiveness amount has not been credited, if the loan is being repaid in full due to cessation, or if your customer doesnГўВҖВҷt qualify for or want forgiveness for any other reason.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Cumulative Interest Owing after this payment
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the appropriate value. The value reported should be equal to the sum of all interest accrued minus the sum of all interest payments.
ГўВ‘В©
Monthly Interest Applicable
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the total amount of interest that has accrued for the loan within the reporting period.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Repayment Category Indicator
ГўВҖВ“ This field isnГўВҖВҷt amendable and defaults to
Non Anomalous
.
ГўВ‘В«
Indicator of Payments Arrears as at month end
ГўВҖВ“ This field defaults to ГўВҖВңNAГўВҖВқ until December 31, 2023, since the loan canГўВҖВҷt be classified as in arrears until after this date, and then only if the borrower doesnГўВҖВҷt begin making payments. If a loan hasnГўВҖВҷt been repaid in full by December 31, 2023, and the applicant doesnГўВҖВҷt begin making payments on the loan, select the number of days that payments on the loan are in arrears as at month end (beginning in January 2024). If arrears are more than 90 days, continue to select
90
.
ГўВ‘В¬
Cessation Event Indicator
ГўВҖВ“ Only select this check box if a cessation event has occurred. Once a cessation event has been triggered, you must continue to select this box in subsequent reporting periods unless the loan is being repaid in full.
ГўВ‘Вӯ
Cessation Event Reason
ГўВҖВ“ Select a reason for the cessation from the drop-down menu. See table for further details.
Reason for Cessation Event
Explanation
Additional Information
All obligations satisfied
The borrower is eligible for forgiveness as all obligations have been fully satisfied. Select this option if the borrower has repaid the loan before December 31, 2023.
No further information required. This cessation event should only be reported when also reporting a loan status at month end of ГўВҖВңFully repaidГўВҖВқ.
Ineligibility-related cessation event
The borrower isnГўВҖВҷt eligible for the CEBA loan and forgiveness, as obligations have not been fully satisfied and all deadlines have passed (e.g., failure to remediate, ineligibility). Select this option if the borrower received funds but ultimately didnГўВҖВҷt qualify for the CEBA loan (and will be assigned to an anomalous repayment group).
Indicate if the loan is for a borrowing or non-borrowing customer. You must continue to report the cessation event and the reason in subsequent reporting periods while the loan is outstanding.
Any other cessation event
The borrower may be eligible for forgiveness, assuming obligations have been fully met. Select this option if cessation is due to insolvency, liquidation, wind-up, or any type of default that triggers cessation.
Indicate if the loan is for a borrowing or non-borrowing member. You must continue to report the cessation event and the reason in subsequent reporting periods while the loan is outstanding (e.g., during a collections process).
ГўВ‘В®
Select
Submit
.
Figure 3: Completing the CEBA Program Repayment Form
END
